seasonArc:
Jonathan Milan's 2026 was the year his sprint pure-form converted at WorldTour scale. UAE Tour delivered the early-season template: Stage 4 win in Fujairah, Stage 5 win in Dubai, four sprint wins by week's end across the team's lead-out machine. Tirreno-Adriatico put him 5th in the Stage 1 ITT (29 seconds back on Ganna), then 2nd on Stage 3's sprint behind Lund Andresen. At Sanremo he was 4th — the textbook Sanremo near-miss, stuck in the bunch when the Cipressa selection went.

The Classics block had him as Lidl-Trek's secondary sprint card behind Pedersen's Classics leadership; the cobbled selection turned every day into a Pedersen day. Then the Giro: 4th and 2nd on the first two flat sprints, beaten by Soudal's Paul Magnier each time. The pattern by mid-Giro was clear — Milan's sprint train works, his leg works, but Magnier's lead-out from the front of the bunch is one step cleaner. The Giro continues; the Tour in July is the year's bigger sprint test.
